contract.name: v2/agency/toptier_code/def_code.md
contract.description: Documented agency DEF code listing route currently responds 404 Not Found with an HTML body for every path/query combination tested.

toptier_code.description: Identifier inserted into the documented path segment; observed 404 for valid (086, 097) and invalid (000) values.
toptier_code.constraints: Required path segment per documentation; live service returns 404 regardless of value.

outputSchema.description: HTML Not Found page (Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8) returned instead of the documented JSON payload.

response.bodyExcerpt:
Not Found

The requested resource was not found on this server.

Not Found

The requested resource was not found on this server.

quirks: Route is undeployed or removed; every probe returns HTML 404 regardless of query parameters or trailing slash variations.
Provider still serves other agency endpoints (e.g., /api/v2/agency/{toptier_code}/) and global DEF reference data, so the failure is specific to this path.
risks: Integrations that rely on the documented JSON schema will fail outright with 404 responses and must defensively handle HTML error bodies.
Until the provider publishes a replacement route, clients cannot retrieve agency-level DEF financial totals programmatically.
gaps: Documentation describes query parameters (fiscal_year, filter, order, sort, page, limit) and JSON payload fields, but none can be validated because the route never returns JSON.

mismatches: Documentation promises JSON Disaster Emergency Fund totals for GET /api/v2/agency/{toptier_code}/def_code/, but the live service responds with HTML 404 Not Found for all tested inputs.
gaps: Documentation describes query parameters (fiscal_year, filter, order, sort, page, limit) and JSON payload fields, but none can be validated because the route never returns JSON.

Until the provider publishes a replacement route, clients cannot retrieve agency-level DEF financial totals programmatically.
